profile.name: Alex Mercer
profile.intro: I build production-ready interfaces with a strong bias toward maintainable architecture, performance, and clear developer experience.
profile.availability: Open to senior frontend and full-stack roles
profile.tagline: Classic developer portfolio with git-flavored UI and modular React architecture.

about: I design frontend systems that stay readable six months later, not only on launch day.
My preferred workflow combines component discipline, type-safe content models, and visual systems that reflect the product’s identity instead of generic templates.
This portfolio uses a git-inspired layout language: commit timelines, branch markers, diff framing, and terminal accents translated into a polished presentation.

skills.title: Frontend
skills.focus: Interfaces that are resilient, accessible, and fast.

skills.title: Product Engineering
skills.focus: Clear abstractions and scalable implementation patterns.

experience.summary: Owned architecture and delivery for customer-facing web platforms with a focus on maintainable component systems.
achievements: Led a React platform refactor that reduced duplicated UI logic across multiple product surfaces.
Introduced typed content and configuration patterns that improved maintainability for non-trivial interfaces.
Worked closely with design and backend peers to ship production features with fewer release regressions.

experience.summary: Built internal and external tools with a bias toward usable interfaces and dependable delivery.
achievements: Created reusable UI patterns for data-dense dashboards and project workflows.
Improved page performance and perceived responsiveness across several core experiences.
Collaborated on release processes and frontend quality practices for a growing engineering team.

projects.title: MergeBoard
projects.summary: A collaborative engineering dashboard that consolidates release status, review state, and deployment signals.
projects.description: MergeBoard is framed like a command center for active delivery. It brings pull request health, deploy checkpoints, and ownership context into a single interface designed for quick scanning and low friction handoffs.
highlights: Structured the UI around reusable data panels and typed content contracts.
Designed a visual system that balanced technical density with readability.
Prepared the app for static deployment and containerized delivery.

projects.title: Branchline
projects.summary: A project showcase site with git-inspired storytelling and modular content architecture.
projects.description: Branchline explores how engineering identity can shape presentation without becoming gimmicky. The site uses commit timelines, branch markers, and diff-inspired content cards to tell project stories while remaining clean and professional.
highlights: Modeled all content with data classes for clean rendering boundaries.
Split sections into maintainable page modules instead of a single monolithic file.
Focused on production-ready frontend structure rather than static mockup quality alone.

projects.title: DeployKit
projects.summary: A frontend starter optimized for shipping static apps with containers and sensible defaults.
projects.description: DeployKit packages a pragmatic frontend baseline: typed routes, reusable sections, deployment-focused docs, and a clean Docker + Nginx path. It is aimed at teams that want fewer decisions between scaffold and production.
highlights: Used a multi-stage Docker strategy to keep the runtime image lean.
Set up clean route fallback behavior for SPA navigation.
Kept the app structure modular for long-term maintenance.

gitHighlights.label: HEAD
gitHighlights.title: Architecture first
gitHighlights.detail: The portfolio is organized around typed content models, reusable section components, and isolated page concerns.

gitHighlights.label: DIFF
gitHighlights.title: Readable implementation
gitHighlights.detail: UI modules are separated by responsibility so visual changes do not require chasing through one giant component file.

gitHighlights.label: BRANCH
gitHighlights.title: Project detail routes
gitHighlights.detail: The landing page stays concise while deeper project context lives in dedicated routes with the same content source.
